question: predict sign of next-month SPY return from implied (VIX) + realized vol at 1/3/6m
data_source: yfinance; implied vol = VIX only; realized vol = SPY 21/63/126d

strategies.reason: monthly directional sign not predictable from VRP; both model and baseline below always-long accuracy (0.68) and AUC~0.5; trading Sharpe 0.41 < buy-and-hold 0.64. VRP predicts equity-premium magnitude at quarterly horizon, not monthly sign.

summary: User-directed test: can VIX/realized-vol variance risk premium predict next-month SPY direction? Answer: NO. Robust negative on a 194-month OOS sample (VIX-only). Always-long (68% up) is unbeaten; trading Sharpe 0.41 < SPY 0.64.
infra_changes: new implied-volatility (VIX) data integration via data.get_prices inside generate_weights
expanding-window walk-forward logistic ML scaffold (look-ahead-safe), reusable
next_angles: predict magnitude/scale exposure rather than sign
test quarterly horizon where VRP predictability is documented
standing_recommendation: voltarget_spy (~76% SPY / 24% cash) remains the only validated strategy
